steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is 'Such lists,' which is plural. The phrase 'observed sharply yet simply by Shōnagon, who served as a lady-in-waiting to Empress Teishi in the tenth century' is a modifier — it does not add another subject. The verb must agree with the plural subject 'lists.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — reveals: singular verb form. Does not agree with the plural subject 'Such lists.' ✗
  • Choice B — was revealing: singular past-progressive verb phrase. Does not agree with the plural subject. ✗
  • Choice C — to reveal: an infinitive, not a conjugated verb. Cannot serve as the main verb of the sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — reveal: plural present-tense verb. Correctly agrees with the plural subject 'Such lists.' ✓
That matches Choice D — reveal.
